rootCause: The NullReferenceException is thrown because the PaymentService.ProcessPayment method attempts to access the Customer property before it has been initialised. The dependency injection container is not registering the ICustomerRepository, so the constructor receives null.
fixSuggestions: Register ICustomerRepository in your DI container: services.AddScoped<ICustomerRepository, CustomerRepository>()
Add a null check in ProcessPayment before accessing Customer properties
Verify your appsettings.json has the correct database connection string for the CustomerRepository
relatedDocs: Microsoft Docs: Dependency Injection in ASP.NET Core
NullReferenceException troubleshooting guide on Stack Overflow
nextStepSuggestion: Run a dependency audit to check if your packages are up to date and compatible.
nextStepDescription: Your error may be related to package version incompatibilities. The Dependency Audit tool can check your project's packages for outdated or vulnerable dependencies.
